chapter_context.summary: Elisha makes a lost iron ax head float, reveals Syrian troop movements, blinds an invading Syrian force, and feeds them; later, Ben-hadad besieges Samaria, causing an extreme, cannibalistic famine.

outline.text: Elisha Causes the Lost Iron Ax Head to Float in Jordan
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elisha Causes the Lost Iron Ax Head to Float in Jordan (6:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Elisha Reveals Syrian Military Movements to King of Israel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elisha Reveals Syrian Military Movements to King of Israel (6:8-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Elisha's Servant Sees Chariots of Fire: 'They that be with us are more'
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elisha's Servant Sees Chariots of Fire: 'They that be with us are more' (6:15-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Elisha Blinds Syrian Forces; Leads Them to Samaria; Feeds and Releases Them
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elisha Blinds Syrian Forces; Leads Them to Samaria; Feeds and Releases Them (6:18-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Ben-hadad Besieges Samaria; Extreme Famine; King Blames Elisha
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Ben-hadad Besieges Samaria; Extreme Famine; King Blames Elisha (6:24-33), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

key_themes.title: Prophetic Authority
key_themes.description: Elisha carries the mantle of Elijah, showing that God's word stands firm in judgment and mercy regardless of political power.

key_themes.title: Covenant Faithfulness and Exile
key_themes.description: The tragic fall of Samaria and Jerusalem shows that persistent rebellion and idolatry inevitably lead to judgment.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 6 prompt you to trust in God's promises even during seasons of spiritual decline?
What warnings or instructions does this chapter hold for guarding your own heart against syncretism?

cultural_historical.title: The Assyrian and Babylonian Empires
cultural_historical.text: The rise of Mesopotamian super-empires (Assyria and Babylon) serves as the historical backdrop for the exile and division of the Hebrew kingdoms.
